figures.caption: Figure 5: The raw text content extracted from the input PDF document used in the evaluation case study. This document features complex hierarchical structures (Chapters, Articles, Clauses) and domain-specific terminology (e.g., "L8 level", "Zero Trust").
Figure 3: Complexity-Aware Token Consumption Analysis. TopoChunker dynamically adjusts token expenditure based on document complexity, achieving lower average costs than static agentic baselines.

Figure 4: Qualitative Examples of TopoChunker output resolving contextual fragmentation. (a) On the GutenQA dataset, the lack of heading hierarchy triggers Path 2: Semantic Flow. TopoChunker actively resolves semantic islands by explicitly mapping dangling pronouns to their ancestral entities in the Context Supplement. (b) On the GovReport dataset, explicit hierarchies trigger Path 1: Structural Rule. Generic references are successfully resolved to specific entities, ensuring chunks remain semantically self-contained for downstream RAG.
Table 3: System Prompts for the Inspector and Refiner Agents.

Figure 7: The structured JSONL output generated by TopoChunker. TopoChunker moves beyond trivial dictionary definitions to perform cross-chunk semantic resolution—such as automatically fetching the Article 1 definitions for Level 1 Data and Level 2 Data when processing Article 2—significantly mitigating retrieval ambiguity for downstream LLMs. Furthermore, because the source document features a large number of tables and complex layouts, the Inspector Agent adaptively routes it through Path 3: Visual Topology, ensuring the structural integrity of these non-textual elements.

Figure 1: The overall architecture of TopoChunker. The dual-agent framework operates across three layers: Cognitive Perception (Inspector Agent for adaptive routing), Execution (Topological Pruner and SIR construction), and Audit (Refiner Agent for context disambiguation). These layers interact via a shared Structured Intermediate Representation (SIR) to form a closed-loop Diagnosis-Execution-Audit pipeline.

Figure 6: The standardized Structured Intermediate Representation (SIR) generated in Stage 2. The parser has successfully mapped visual font cues to a logical Markdown stream (#, ##, ###), preserving the hierarchical skeleton for the downstream Refiner Agent.

Table 1: Domain-Specific RAG Performance. Performance is reported using Recall@k (R@k) and DCG@k (D@k). TopoChunker demonstrates consistently superior retrieval accuracy across both unstructured and highly structured domains.
